title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: lintrhelper makes easy create custom linters lintr package. best part? don’t need know XPath understand XML parse trees! vignette show create powerful linters using simple, intuitive functions.

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("fabiandistler/lintrhelper") library(lintrhelper)

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: lintr comes many built-linters, might want create custom linters : Team-specific style guides: Enforce conventions unique team organization Domain-specific rules: Check patterns specific field (e.g., statistical best practices) Package-specific warnings: Flag deprecated functions suggest package-specific alternatives Project conventions: Enforce naming conventions, file structure rules, etc.

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: Let’s create linter warns using T F instead TRUE FALSE: ’s ! XPath, XML, just plain R.
code: # Just list the symbols you want to forbid! no_t_f <- forbid_symbols(   c("T", "F"),   "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of {symbol}." )  # Test it test_linter(no_t_f, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(no_t_f, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: symbols: character vector variable names forbid message: message show users. {symbol} gets replaced actual symbol found type: (optional) Severity level - “style”, “warning”, “error”

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: XPath query language XML. R code parsed XML tree, linters use XPath find problematic patterns.

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: examples: //SYMBOL - symbols (variable names) //SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL - Function calls //SYMBOL[text() = 'my_var'] - Specific symbol //NUM_CONST - Numeric constants //STR_CONST - String constants
code: # View all common patterns xpath_patterns()  # View specific category xpath_patterns("functions")

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: Use quick_test() rapidly test XPath expressions:
code: quick_test("//SYMBOL[text() = 'T']", "x <- T") # Shows if the XPath matches and where

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: common use case flagging specific function calls: can flag multiple functions: Note {function} placeholder - replaced actual function name found.
code: no_sapply <- create_function_call_linter(   function_names = "sapply",   message = "Use vapply() instead of {function} for type-safe code.",   linter_name = "no_sapply_linter" )  # Test it test_linter(no_sapply, "result <- sapply(1:10, sqrt)", should_lint = TRUE) deprecated_funcs <- create_function_call_linter(   function_names = c("sapply", "mapply"),   message = "Function {function} is deprecated in our style guide.",   linter_name = "deprecated_functions" )

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: Another common pattern enforcing assignment operator style:
code: prefer_arrow <- create_assignment_linter(   forbidden_operators = "=",   message = "Use <- for assignment, not =.",   linter_name = "prefer_arrow_assignment",   type = "style" )  test_linter(prefer_arrow, "x = 5", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(prefer_arrow, "x <- 5", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: test_linter() function provides several testing modes:
code: # Basic: does it lint? test_linter(my_linter, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE)  # Expect no lints test_linter(my_linter, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)  # Check exact number of lints test_linter(my_linter, "a <- T; b <- T", n_lints = 2)  # Verify message content test_linter(   my_linter,   "x <- T",   message_pattern = "TRUE/FALSE" )

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: Get started quickly templates:
code: # View all templates linter_template("all")  # Get specific template linter_template("simple") linter_template("function_call") linter_template("assignment") linter_template("advanced")

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: package includes several example linters can use learn :
code: # Ready-to-use linters lintr::lint("my_script.R", linters = no_t_f_linter()) lintr::lint("my_script.R", linters = no_attach_linter()) lintr::lint("my_script.R", linters = prefer_arrow_assignment_linter()) lintr::lint("my_script.R", linters = no_sapply_linter())

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: complex logic, can build linters using full lintr API:
code: library(lintr) library(xml2)  advanced_linter <- function() {   lintr::Linter(function(source_expression) {     if (!lintr::is_lint_level(source_expression, "file")) {       return(list())     }      xml <- source_expression$full_xml_parsed_content      # Find all function calls     nodes <- xml2::xml_find_all(xml, "//SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL")      # Custom filtering logic     bad_nodes <- Filter(function(node) {       func_name <- xml2::xml_text(node)       # Flag functions starting with "old_"       grepl("^old_", func_name)     }, nodes)      # Generate custom lints     lints <- lapply(bad_nodes, function(node) {       func_name <- xml2::xml_text(node)       new_name <- sub("^old_", "new_", func_name)        lintr::xml_nodes_to_lints(         node,         source_expression = source_expression,         lint_message = sprintf(           "Function %s is deprecated. Use %s instead.",           func_name, new_name         ),         type = "warning"       )     })      unlist(lints, recursive = FALSE)   }) }

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: ’ve created linters, use lintr:
code: # Single file lintr::lint("my_script.R", linters = my_linter())  # Multiple linters lintr::lint(   "my_script.R",   linters = lintr::linters_with_defaults(     my_linter = my_linter(),     another_linter = another_linter()   ) )  # Entire package lintr::lint_package(   linters = lintr::linters_with_defaults(     my_linter = my_linter()   ) )

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: Start simple: Begin create_simple_linter() XPath Test thoroughly: Use test_linter() various code examples Use quick_test(): Iterate quickly developing XPath expressions Clear messages: Write helpful, actionable lint messages Appropriate severity: Use “style” preferences, “warning” potential issues, “error” serious problems Document linters: Explain pattern problematic

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: Read lintr creating linters vignette Learn XPath Explore source code built-lintr linters Share useful linters community!

title: Getting Started with lintrhelper
text: encounter issues questions: Check ?create_simple_linter function documentation Use linter_template() xpath_patterns() reference Visit GitHub repository

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Creating custom linters lintr traditionally requires: Understanding R code parsed XML tree Learning XPath query syntax Navigating complex XML node structures Understanding relationship R syntax XML elements significant barrier R users just want enforce simple coding standards.

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: lintrhelper provides high-level functions abstract away XPath complexity. describe want lint, find parse tree.
code: library(lintrhelper)

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Ban T/F, single-letter variables, reserved names
code: # Ban T and F no_t_f <- forbid_symbols(   c("T", "F"),   "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of {symbol}." )  # Ban all single-letter variable names no_single_letters <- forbid_symbols(   letters,   "Avoid single-letter variable '{symbol}'." )  # Ban specific reserved words for your project no_reserved <- forbid_symbols(   c("tmp", "temp", "data", "result"),   "'{symbol}' is too generic. Use a more descriptive name." )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Ban deprecated discouraged functions
code: # Ban attach() no_attach <- forbid_functions(   "attach",   "Don't use {function}(). Use with() or explicit $ notation instead." )  # Ban multiple functions at once no_apply_family <- forbid_functions(   c("sapply", "mapply", "tapply"),   "Use purrr or vapply() instead of {function}()." )  # Suggest alternatives automatically prefer_vapply <- forbid_functions(   "sapply",   alternatives = "vapply"   # Auto-generates: "Use vapply() instead of sapply()." )  # Multiple alternatives prefer_tidyverse <- forbid_functions(   "subset",   alternatives = c("dplyr::filter", "dplyr::select") )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Require snake_case, camelCase, patterns
code: # Require snake_case for variables snake_case <- require_naming_pattern(   "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$",   "Variable '{symbol}' should use snake_case." )  # Require camelCase camel_case <- require_naming_pattern(   "^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$",   "Variable '{symbol}' should use camelCase." )  # Forbid names starting with uppercase (use invert=TRUE) no_uppercase_start <- require_naming_pattern(   "^[A-Z]",   "Variable '{symbol}' should not start with uppercase.",   invert = TRUE )  # Forbid underscores no_underscores <- require_naming_pattern(   "_",   "Variable '{symbol}' should not contain underscores.",   invert = TRUE )  # Require specific prefixes require_prefix <- require_naming_pattern(   "^(m_|g_|l_)",   "Variable '{symbol}' should start with scope prefix (m_, g_, or l_)." )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Require verbs, specific patterns, avoid patterns
code: # Functions should start with verbs verb_functions <- require_function_naming_pattern(   "^(get|set|calculate|compute|check|is|has|create|update|delete|find|load|save|validate)",   "Function '{function}' should start with a verb." )  # No "helper" or "util" in function names no_generic_names <- require_function_naming_pattern(   "helper|util|misc|other",   "Function '{function}' should have a more descriptive name.",   invert = TRUE )  # API functions must start with "api_" api_prefix <- require_function_naming_pattern(   "^api_",   "Public API function '{function}' must start with 'api_'." )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Standardize <-, =, ->
code: # Prefer <- (most common in R) use_arrow <- enforce_assignment_operator("<-")  # Prefer = (some teams prefer this) use_equals <- enforce_assignment_operator("=")  # The function automatically generates appropriate messages

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Ensure critical arguments always specified
code: # Always specify stringsAsFactors in data.frame() explicit_saf <- require_function_arguments(   "data.frame",   "stringsAsFactors",   "Always specify stringsAsFactors explicitly." )  # Require na.rm in mean() explicit_na_rm <- require_function_arguments(   "mean",   "na.rm",   "Always specify na.rm in mean()." )  # Multiple required arguments explicit_args <- require_function_arguments(   "read.csv",   c("stringsAsFactors", "header"),   "Always specify stringsAsFactors and header in read.csv()." )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use case: Maintain readable code width
code: # Standard 80 characters line_length_80 <- limit_line_length(80)  # Strict 72 characters (for emails, etc.) line_length_72 <- limit_line_length(72)  # Lenient 120 characters line_length_120 <- limit_line_length(120)

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Combine multiple linters comprehensive style guide:
code: # Define your team's rules my_team_style <- lintr::linters_with_defaults(   # No T/F   no_t_f = forbid_symbols(c("T", "F"), "Use TRUE/FALSE")(),    # Require snake_case   snake_case = require_naming_pattern(     "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$",     "Use snake_case"   )(),    # Prefer <-   use_arrow = enforce_assignment_operator("<-")(),    # Ban dangerous functions   no_attach = forbid_functions("attach", alternatives = "with")(),    # Ban deprecated apply functions   no_sapply = forbid_functions(     c("sapply", "mapply"),     alternatives = "vapply"   )(),    # Functions must start with verbs   verb_functions = require_function_naming_pattern(     "^(get|set|calc|check|is|has)",     "Functions should start with verbs"   )(),    # Explicit arguments   explicit_saf = require_function_arguments(     "data.frame",     "stringsAsFactors"   )(),    # Line length   line_length = limit_line_length(80)() )  # Apply to a file lintr::lint("script.R", linters = my_team_style)  # Apply to entire package lintr::lint_package(linters = my_team_style)

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: Use test_linter() verify linters work correctly:
code: my_linter <- forbid_symbols(c("T", "F"), "Use TRUE/FALSE")  # Should lint test_linter(my_linter, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE)  # Should not lint test_linter(my_linter, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)  # Check exact number of lints test_linter(my_linter, "a <- T; b <- F", n_lints = 2)  # Verify message content test_linter(   my_linter,   "x <- T",   message_pattern = "TRUE/FALSE" )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: complex scenarios can’t handled high-level functions, can still use XPath-based linters: 95% use cases, high-level functions need!
code: # For advanced users only complex_linter <- create_simple_linter(   xpath = "//OP-COLON[preceding-sibling::NUM_CONST[text() = '1']]",   message = "Use seq_along() instead of 1:length()",   linter_name = "no_one_length" )

title: Creating Linters Without XPath
text: lintrhelper makes creating custom linters accessible everyone: Start linting code today - XPath knowledge required!

what: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: Helper Functions Creating Custom Lintr Rules - XPath Knowledge Required! lintrhelper makes incredibly easy create custom linters lintr package. don’t need know XPath, XML, parse trees - just use simple, intuitive functions describe want lint! 📖 Documentation site: https://fabiandistler.github.io/lintrhelper/

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: Install development version GitHub either pak (recommended) devtools:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("fabiandistler/lintrhelper")  # or # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("fabiandistler/lintrhelper")

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: Three lines ban T/F R script: ’s . rest README walks building blocks.
code: library(lintrhelper) no_t_f <- forbid_symbols(c("T", "F"), "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of {symbol}.") lintr::lint("script.R", linters = list(no_t_f()))

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: Creating custom linters lintr typically involves: - Understanding R’s XML parse tree structure - Writing complex XPath expressions - Handling source expressions XML nodes - Setting proper testing lintrhelper eliminates complexity! Just describe want plain terms.

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: library(lintrhelper)  # Ban T and F - just list them! no_t_f <- forbid_symbols(   c("T", "F"),   "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of {symbol}." )  # Test it test_linter(no_t_f, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(no_t_f, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)  # Use with lintr lintr::lint("my_script.R", linters = no_t_f())

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Ban dangerous functions - just name them! no_attach <- forbid_functions(   "attach",   "Don't use {function}(). Use with() instead." )  # Or suggest alternatives automatically no_sapply <- forbid_functions(   "sapply",   alternatives = "vapply"  # Auto-generates helpful message! )  # Ban multiple functions at once no_deprecated <- forbid_functions(   c("sapply", "mapply", "tapply"),   "Function {function}() is discouraged." )

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Require snake_case - just give a regex pattern! snake_case <- require_naming_pattern(   "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$",   "Variable '{symbol}' should use snake_case." )  test_linter(snake_case, "myVar <- 1", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(snake_case, "my_var <- 1", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Prefer <- (the most common style) use_arrow <- enforce_assignment_operator("<-")  test_linter(use_arrow, "x = 5", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(use_arrow, "x <- 5", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: functions let create linters without XPath knowledge: forbid_symbols() - Ban specific variable names forbid_functions() - Ban specific function calls (optional alternatives) require_naming_pattern() - Enforce naming conventions regex require_function_naming_pattern() - Enforce function naming conventions enforce_assignment_operator() - Prefer <-, =, -> require_function_arguments() - Ensure functions called specific arguments limit_line_length() - Enforce maximum line length

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: test_linter() - Simplified testing linters quick_test() - One-liner rapid testing

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: advanced users want control: create_simple_linter() - Build XPath-based linters create_function_call_linter() - XPath-based function call linters create_assignment_linter() - XPath-based assignment linters linter_template() - Code templates xpath_patterns() - XPath reference guide

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: no_t_f_linter(), no_attach_linter(), prefer_arrow_assignment_linter(), no_sapply_linter(), no_one_length_linter()

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # See all available templates linter_template("all")  # Get specific template linter_template("function_call") linter_template("advanced")

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # View common XPath patterns xpath_patterns()  # See specific pattern category xpath_patterns("functions") xpath_patterns("operators")

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # No XPath needed - just list the letters! no_single_letters <- forbid_symbols(   letters,  # a, b, c, ..., z   "Avoid single-letter variable name '{symbol}'." )  test_linter(no_single_letters, "x <- 5", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(no_single_letters, "count <- 5", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Functions should start with verbs verb_functions <- require_function_naming_pattern(   "^(get|set|calculate|compute|check|is|has|create|update|delete|find|load|save)",   "Function '{function}' should start with a verb." )  test_linter(verb_functions, "result <- process()", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(verb_functions, "result <- calculate_total()", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Use invert=TRUE to forbid patterns that DO match no_temp_names <- require_naming_pattern(   "temp",   "Variable '{symbol}' should not contain 'temp'.",   invert = TRUE )  test_linter(no_temp_names, "temp_var <- 1", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(no_temp_names, "result <- 1", should_lint = FALSE)

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Always specify stringsAsFactors explicit_saf <- require_function_arguments(   "data.frame",   "stringsAsFactors",   "Always specify stringsAsFactors explicitly in data.frame()." )  # Lints this: test_linter(explicit_saf, "df <- data.frame(x = 1:3)", should_lint = TRUE)  # Passes this: test_linter(   explicit_saf,   "df <- data.frame(x = 1:3, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)",   should_lint = FALSE )

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
code: # Combine multiple rules for your team my_team_linters <- lintr::linters_with_defaults(   no_t_f = forbid_symbols(c("T", "F"), "Use TRUE/FALSE")(),   snake_case = require_naming_pattern("^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$", "Use snake_case")(),   use_arrow = enforce_assignment_operator("<-")(),   no_attach = forbid_functions("attach", alternatives = "with")(),   line_length = limit_line_length(80)() )  # Apply to your project lintr::lint_package(linters = my_team_linters)

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: test_linter() function provides several ways verify linters:
code: my_linter <- create_simple_linter(   xpath = "//SYMBOL[text() = 'T']",   message = "Don't use T",   linter_name = "no_t" )  # Basic: should it lint or not? test_linter(my_linter, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(my_linter, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)  # Check exact number of lints test_linter(my_linter, "a <- T; b <- T", n_lints = 2)  # Check message content test_linter(   my_linter,   "x <- T",   should_lint = TRUE,   message_pattern = "Don't use" )

title: Create Custom Lintr Rules Without XPath Knowledge
text: developing XPath expressions, use quick_test():
code: # Test if your XPath matches what you expect quick_test("//SYMBOL[text() = 'T']", "x <- T") # Found 1 lint(s): # <text>:1:6: warning: [temp_test_linter] Found match # x <- T #      ^  quick_test("//SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL[text() = 'mean']", "x <- mean(y)") # Found 1 lint(s): ...

title: Create an Assignment Linter — create_assignment_linter
text: Helper creating linters check assignment patterns.

title: Create an Assignment Linter — create_assignment_linter
code: create_assignment_linter(   forbidden_operators,   message,   linter_name,   type = c("style", "warning", "error") )

title: Create an Assignment Linter — create_assignment_linter
text: forbidden_operators Character vector forbidden assignment operators (e.g., c("=", "<<-")). message lint message display. linter_name character string naming linter. type type lint. Defaults "style".

title: Create an Assignment Linter — create_assignment_linter
text: linter function.

title: Create an Assignment Linter — create_assignment_linter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Enforce <- over = no_equals_assignment_linter <- create_assignment_linter(   forbidden_operators = "=",   message = "Use <- for assignment, not =.",   linter_name = "no_equals_assignment" ) } # }

what: Create a Function Call Linter — create_function_call_linter
title: Create a Function Call Linter — create_function_call_linter
text: Specialized helper creating linters target specific function calls. common pattern linting, function makes even easier.

title: Create a Function Call Linter — create_function_call_linter
code: create_function_call_linter(   function_names,   message,   linter_name,   type = c("warning", "error", "style") )

title: Create a Function Call Linter — create_function_call_linter
text: function_names Character vector function names lint . message character string lint message. Use function placeholder actual function name found. linter_name character string naming linter. type type lint. Defaults "warning".

title: Create a Function Call Linter — create_function_call_linter
text: linter function.

title: Create a Function Call Linter — create_function_call_linter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Warn against using paste0 in favor of paste no_paste0_linter <- create_function_call_linter(   function_names = "paste0",   message = "Use paste(..., sep = '') instead of {function}.",   linter_name = "no_paste0_linter" ) } # }

what: Create a Simple XPath-Based Linter — create_simple_linter
title: Create a Simple XPath-Based Linter — create_simple_linter
text: function simplifies creation custom linters using XPath expressions. wraps standard lintr pattern finding XML nodes converting lints.

title: Create a Simple XPath-Based Linter — create_simple_linter
code: create_simple_linter(   xpath,   message,   linter_name,   type = c("warning", "error", "style") )

title: Create a Simple XPath-Based Linter — create_simple_linter
text: xpath character string containing XPath expression match problematic code. message character string lint message display. Can use function placeholder replaced function name applicable. linter_name character string naming linter (used lint type). type type lint create. Defaults "warning". Can "error", "warning", "style".

title: Create a Simple XPath-Based Linter — create_simple_linter
text: linter function can used lintr.

title: Create a Simple XPath-Based Linter — create_simple_linter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a linter that warns against using T/F instead of TRUE/FALSE tf_linter <- create_simple_linter(   xpath = "//SYMBOL[text() = 'T' or text() = 'F']",   message = "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of T/F.",   linter_name = "tf_linter" )  # Use it with lintr lintr::lint("x <- T", linters = tf_linter()) } # }

title: Enforce Assignment Operator — enforce_assignment_operator
text: Simple way enforce specific assignment operator without XPath.

title: Enforce Assignment Operator — enforce_assignment_operator
code: enforce_assignment_operator(   prefer = c("<-", "=", "->"),   message = NULL,   type = c("style", "warning", "error") )

title: Enforce Assignment Operator — enforce_assignment_operator
text: prefer operator prefer: "<-", "=", "->". message Optional custom message. type lint type. Defaults "style".

title: Enforce Assignment Operator — enforce_assignment_operator
text: linter function.

title: Enforce Assignment Operator — enforce_assignment_operator
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Prefer <- (most common) use_arrow <- enforce_assignment_operator("<-")  # Prefer = use_equals <- enforce_assignment_operator("=")  test_linter(use_arrow, "x = 5", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(use_arrow, "x <- 5", should_lint = FALSE) } # }

title: Example Linters — example_linters

title: Example Linters — example_linters
code: no_t_f_linter()  no_attach_linter()  prefer_arrow_assignment_linter()  no_one_length_linter()  no_sapply_linter()

title: Example Linters — example_linters
text: no_t_f_linter(): Warns using T/F instead TRUE/FALSE no_attach_linter(): Warns using attach() prefer_arrow_assignment_linter(): Suggests using <- instead = assignment no_one_length_linter(): Warns 1:length(x) pattern no_sapply_linter(): Style guide: warns sapply

title: Forbid Specific Functions — forbid_functions
text: Create linter flags specific function calls. simplified version require XPath knowledge.

title: Forbid Specific Functions — forbid_functions
code: forbid_functions(   functions,   message = NULL,   alternatives = NULL,   type = c("warning", "error", "style") )

title: Forbid Specific Functions — forbid_functions
text: functions Character vector function names forbid. message lint message. Use function placeholder. alternatives Optional character vector alternative functions suggest. type lint type. Defaults "warning".

title: Forbid Specific Functions — forbid_functions
text: linter function.

title: Forbid Specific Functions — forbid_functions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Ban attach() no_attach <- forbid_functions(   "attach",   "Don't use {function}(). Use with() instead." )  # With alternatives no_sapply <- forbid_functions(   "sapply",   alternatives = "vapply" )  # Multiple functions no_deprecated <- forbid_functions(   c("sapply", "mapply", "tapply"),   "Function {function}() is discouraged." ) } # }

title: Forbid Specific Symbols (Variable Names) — forbid_symbols
text: Create linter flags specific symbol names. Useful banning certain variable names enforcing naming conventions.

title: Forbid Specific Symbols (Variable Names) — forbid_symbols
code: forbid_symbols(   symbols,   message = "Symbol '{symbol}' should not be used.",   type = c("style", "warning", "error") )

title: Forbid Specific Symbols (Variable Names) — forbid_symbols
text: symbols Character vector symbol names forbid. message lint message. Use symbol placeholder symbol name. type lint type. Defaults "style".

title: Forbid Specific Symbols (Variable Names) — forbid_symbols
text: linter function.

title: Forbid Specific Symbols (Variable Names) — forbid_symbols
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Ban T and F no_t_f <- forbid_symbols(   c("T", "F"),   "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of {symbol}." )  # Ban single-letter variable names no_single_letters <- forbid_symbols(   letters,   "Avoid single-letter variable name '{symbol}'." )  test_linter(no_t_f, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE) } # }

title: Limit Line Length — limit_line_length
text: Flag lines exceed certain character length.

title: Limit Line Length — limit_line_length
code: limit_line_length(   max_length = 80,   message = NULL,   type = c("style", "warning", "error") )

title: Limit Line Length — limit_line_length
text: max_length Maximum allowed line length. Default 80. message lint message. type lint type. Defaults "style".

title: Limit Line Length — limit_line_length
text: linter function.

title: Limit Line Length — limit_line_length
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Standard 80 characters line_length_80 <- limit_line_length(80)  # More strict line_length_72 <- limit_line_length(72) } # }

title: Get Linter Template Code — linter_template
text: Returns template code creating different types linters. helpful getting started custom linters.

title: Get Linter Template Code — linter_template
code: linter_template(   type = c("simple", "function_call", "assignment", "advanced", "all"),   cat_output = TRUE )

title: Get Linter Template Code — linter_template
text: type type template generate. Options include: "simple" - Basic XPath-based linter "function_call" - Function call linter "assignment" - Assignment pattern linter "advanced" - advanced linter custom logic "" - Shows templates cat_output Logical. TRUE (default), prints template console. FALSE, returns template character string.

title: Get Linter Template Code — linter_template
text: cat_output FALSE, returns template code character string.   Otherwise, returns NULL invisibly printing.

title: Get Linter Template Code — linter_template
code: # View a simple linter template linter_template("simple") #>  #> # Simple XPath-based linter example #> library(lintrhelper) #>  #> my_simple_linter <- create_simple_linter( #>   xpath = "//SYMBOL[text() = 'T' or text() = 'F']", #>   message = "Use TRUE/FALSE instead of T/F.", #>   linter_name = "no_t_f_linter", #>   type = "warning" #> ) #>  #> # Test it #> test_linter(my_simple_linter, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE) #> test_linter(my_simple_linter, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)  # Get function call linter template as string code <- linter_template("function_call", cat_output = FALSE)

title: Quick Lint Test — quick_test
text: Even simpler one-liner quick testing development.

title: Quick Lint Test — quick_test
code: quick_test(xpath, code, message = "Found match")

title: Quick Lint Test — quick_test
text: xpath XPath expression test. code Code test . message Message lint (optional, display).

title: Quick Lint Test — quick_test
text: lints found.

title: Quick Lint Test — quick_test
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Quick test if an XPath matches quick_test("//SYMBOL[text() = 'T']", "x <- T") } # }

title: Require Specific Function Arguments — require_function_arguments
text: Check certain functions called specific arguments. Useful enforcing explicit parameter usage.

title: Require Specific Function Arguments — require_function_arguments
code: require_function_arguments(   function_name,   required_args,   message = sprintf("Function %s() should include argument(s): %s", function_name,     paste(required_args, collapse = ", ")),   type = c("warning", "style", "error") )

title: Require Specific Function Arguments — require_function_arguments
text: function_name function check. required_args Character vector required argument names. message lint message. type lint type. Defaults "warning".

title: Require Specific Function Arguments — require_function_arguments
text: linter function.

title: Require Specific Function Arguments — require_function_arguments
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Require explicit stringsAsFactors in data.frame() explicit_saf <- require_function_arguments(   "data.frame",   "stringsAsFactors",   "Always specify stringsAsFactors explicitly in data.frame()." )  # This would lint: # data.frame(x = 1:3)  # This would pass: # data.frame(x = 1:3, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) } # }

title: Require Function Naming Pattern — require_function_naming_pattern
text: Like require_naming_pattern specifically function names.

title: Require Function Naming Pattern — require_function_naming_pattern
code: require_function_naming_pattern(   pattern,   message = "Function '{function}' does not follow naming convention.",   type = c("style", "warning", "error"),   invert = FALSE )

title: Require Function Naming Pattern — require_function_naming_pattern
text: pattern Regular expression function names must match. message lint message. Use function placeholder. type lint type. Defaults "style". invert TRUE, flags names match (forbidding patterns).

title: Require Function Naming Pattern — require_function_naming_pattern
text: linter function.

title: Require Function Naming Pattern — require_function_naming_pattern
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Require functions to start with verb verb_functions <- require_function_naming_pattern(   "^(get|set|calculate|check|is|has|create|update|delete)",   "Function '{function}' should start with a verb." )  # Forbid functions with "temp" in name no_temp_functions <- require_function_naming_pattern(   "temp",   "Function '{function}' should not contain 'temp'.",   invert = TRUE ) } # }

title: Require Naming Pattern — require_naming_pattern
text: Create linter enforces naming conventions symbols (variables). XPath knowledge required - just specify pattern!

title: Require Naming Pattern — require_naming_pattern
code: require_naming_pattern(   pattern,   message = "Name '{symbol}' does not follow naming convention.",   type = c("style", "warning", "error"),   invert = FALSE )

title: Require Naming Pattern — require_naming_pattern
text: pattern Regular expression pattern names must match. message lint message. Use symbol placeholder. type lint type. Defaults "style". invert TRUE, flags names match pattern (forbidding patterns).

title: Require Naming Pattern — require_naming_pattern
text: linter function.

title: Require Naming Pattern — require_naming_pattern
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Require snake_case snake_case_linter <- require_naming_pattern(   "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$",   "Variable '{symbol}' should use snake_case." )  # Forbid names starting with uppercase (invert = TRUE) no_uppercase_start <- require_naming_pattern(   "^[A-Z]",   "Variable '{symbol}' should not start with uppercase.",   invert = TRUE )  test_linter(snake_case_linter, "myVar <- 1", should_lint = TRUE) test_linter(snake_case_linter, "my_var <- 1", should_lint = FALSE) } # }

what: Test a Linter with Example Code — test_linter
title: Test a Linter with Example Code — test_linter
text: Simplified interface testing custom linters. function wraps lintr's testing functionality make easier quickly test custom linters development.

title: Test a Linter with Example Code — test_linter
code: test_linter(   linter,   code,   should_lint = TRUE,   n_lints = NULL,   message_pattern = NULL )

title: Test a Linter with Example Code — test_linter
text: linter linter function (linter factory returns linter). code Character string vector code lint. should_lint Logical. TRUE (default), expects linter find issues. FALSE, expects linter find issues. n_lints Integer. Expected number lints. NULL, just checks linting occurred (occur based should_lint). message_pattern Optional regex pattern match lint messages.

title: Test a Linter with Example Code — test_linter
text: Invisibly returns lints found (list).

title: Test a Linter with Example Code — test_linter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ my_linter <- create_simple_linter(   xpath = "//SYMBOL[text() = 'T']",   message = "Don't use T",   linter_name = "no_t" )  # Test that it catches T test_linter(my_linter, "x <- T", should_lint = TRUE)  # Test that it doesn't flag TRUE test_linter(my_linter, "x <- TRUE", should_lint = FALSE)  # Test exact number of lints test_linter(my_linter, "x <- T; y <- T", n_lints = 2) } # }

what: List Common XPath Patterns for R Code — xpath_patterns
title: List Common XPath Patterns for R Code — xpath_patterns
text: Provides reference useful XPath patterns linting R code.

title: List Common XPath Patterns for R Code — xpath_patterns
code: xpath_patterns(pattern = NULL)

title: List Common XPath Patterns for R Code — xpath_patterns
text: pattern Optional. specified, shows requested pattern. Options: "symbols", "functions", "operators", "literals", "comments".

title: List Common XPath Patterns for R Code — xpath_patterns
text: Prints XPath patterns console.

title: List Common XPath Patterns for R Code — xpath_patterns
code: # See all patterns xpath_patterns() #>  #> === SYMBOLS === #> All symbols: //SYMBOL #> Specific symbol: //SYMBOL[text() = 'my_var'] #> Symbols matching pattern: //SYMBOL[contains(text(), 'temp')]  #>  #> === FUNCTIONS === #> Function calls: //SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL #> Specific function: //SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL[text() = 'mean'] #> Function definitions: //FUNCTION  #>  #> === OPERATORS === #> All assignments: //LEFT_ASSIGN | //RIGHT_ASSIGN | //EQ_ASSIGN #> Left assign (<-): //LEFT_ASSIGN #> Equals assign (=): //EQ_ASSIGN #> Arithmetic ops: //OP-PLUS | //OP-MINUS | //OP-TIMES | //OP-DIVIDE  #>  #> === LITERALS === #> Numeric constants: //NUM_CONST #> String constants: //STR_CONST #> NULL values: //NULL_CONST  #>  #> === COMMENTS === #> All comments: //COMMENT  #>  #> === COMMON_PATTERNS === #> Arguments in function call: //SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL/following-sibling::expr #> Assignment target: //LEFT_ASSIGN/preceding-sibling::expr #> Assignment value: //LEFT_ASSIGN/following-sibling::expr #> If conditions: //IF/following-sibling::expr[1]   # See just function-related patterns xpath_patterns("functions") #> === FUNCTIONS === #> Function calls: //SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL #> Specific function: //SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL[text() = 'mean'] #> Function definitions: //FUNCTION

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0.9000 (development)
text: forbid_symbols(), create_function_call_linter(), require_naming_pattern(), require_function_naming_pattern() now correctly return multiple lints several locations match. Previously per-node messages assembled unlist(recursive = FALSE), broke lints object structure silently dropped results. create_assignment_linter() (therefore enforce_assignment_operator()) now matches = assignments. previous XPath assumed assignment always wrapped <expr>, = lives <expr_or_assign_or_help>. require_naming_pattern() require_function_naming_pattern() honour invert argument — branches conditional returning value. require_function_arguments() now inspects full call expression (grandparent SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL) looking named arguments, calls supply required argument longer trigger. limit_line_length() now scoped file level (used run per expression rely non-existent $lines field).

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0.9000 (development)
text: Added GitHub Actions workflow R CMD check across macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (R devel/release/oldrel-1). Added pkgdown workflow deploys documentation site https://fabiandistler.github.io/lintrhelper/. Added _pkgdown.yml configuration grouping reference index user-facing categories. Generated man/ pages existing roxygen comments (R CMD check previously failed Rd files committed). DESCRIPTION: real author, declared utils import (capture.output), dropped unused rlang import, dropped LazyData (data/ directory).

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0
text: main feature lintrhelper don’t need know XPath create linters: Added forbid_symbols() - Ban specific variable names Added forbid_functions() - Ban function calls auto-generated alternative suggestions Added require_naming_pattern() - Enforce naming conventions regex (XPath!) Added require_function_naming_pattern() - Enforce function naming regex Added enforce_assignment_operator() - Prefer <-, =, -> Added require_function_arguments() - Ensure functions called specific arguments Added limit_line_length() - Enforce maximum line length

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0
text: Added test_linter() simplified linter testing Added quick_test() rapid development

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0
text: users want control: Added create_simple_linter() XPath-based linter creation Added create_function_call_linter() XPath-based function call linters Added create_assignment_linter() XPath-based assignment operator linters Added linter_template() code templates Added xpath_patterns() XPath reference

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0
text: Included example linters: no_t_f_linter(), no_attach_linter(), prefer_arrow_assignment_linter(), no_sapply_linter(), no_one_length_linter()

title: lintrhelper 0.1.0
text: Added comprehensive README emphasizing -XPath approach Added “Getting Started” vignette Added “Creating Linters Without XPath” vignette extensive examples Added unit tests functions
